author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter I. 1–5.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: lectures or tractates

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: gospel according to st.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When I give heed to what we have just read from the apostolic lesson, that “the natural man perceiveth not the things which are of the Spirit of God,” and consider that in the present assembly, my beloved, there must of necessity be among you many natural men, who know only according to the flesh, and cannot yet raise themselves to spiritual understanding, I am in great difficulty how, as the Lord shall grant, I may be able to express, or in my small measure to explain, what has been read from the Gospel, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God;” for this the natural man does not perceive.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then, brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Shall we be silent for this cause?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why then is it read, if we are to be silent regarding it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or why is it heard, if it be not explained?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And why is it explained, if it be not understood?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so, on the other hand, since I do not doubt that there are among your number some who can not only receive it when explained, but even understand it before it is explained, I shall not defraud those who are able to receive it, from fear of my words being wasted on the ears of those who are not able to receive it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Finally, there will be present with us the compassion of God, so that perchance there may be enough for all, and each receive what he is able, while he who speaks says what he is able.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For to speak of the matter as it is, who is able?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I venture to say, my brethren, perhaps not John himself spoke of the matter as it is, but even he only as he was able; for it was man that spoke of God, inspired indeed by God, but still man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because he was inspired he said something; if he had not been inspired, he would have said nothing; but because a man inspired, he spoke not the whole, but what a man could he spoke.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this John, dearly beloved brethren, was one of those mountains concerning which it is written: “Let the mountains receive peace for thy people, and the hills righteousness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The mountains are lofty souls, the hills little souls.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But for this reason do the mountains receive peace, that the hills may be able to receive righteousness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is the righteousness which the hills receive?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Faith, for “the just doth live by faith.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The smaller souls, however, would not receive faith unless the greater souls, which are called mountains, were illuminated by Wisdom herself, that they may be able to transmit to the little ones what the little ones can receive; and the hills live by faith, because the mountains receive peace.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By the mountains themselves it was said to the Church, “Peace be with you;” and the mountains themselves in proclaiming peace to the Church did not divide themselves against Him from whom they received peace, that truly, not feignedly, they might proclaim peace.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there are other mountains which cause shipwreck, on which, if any one drive his ship, she is dashed to pieces.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is easy, when land is seen by men in peril, to make a venture as it were to reach it; but sometimes land is seen on a mountain, and rocks lie hid under the mountain; and when any one makes for the mountain, he falls on the rocks, and finds there not rest, but wrecking.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So there have been certain mountains, and great have they appeared among men, and they have created heresies and schisms, and have divided the Church of God; but those who divided the Church of God were not those mountains concerning which it is said, “Let the mountains receive peace for thy people.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in what manner have they received peace who have severed unity?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But those who received peace to proclaim it to the people have made Wisdom herself an object of contemplation, so far as human hearts could lay hold on that which “eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, neither has ascended into the heart of man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If it has not ascended into the heart of man, how has it ascended into the heart of John?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was not John a man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or perhaps neither into John’s heart did it ascend, but John’s heart ascended into it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For that which ascends into the heart of man is from beneath, to man; but that to which the heart of man ascends is above, from man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even so brethren, can it be said that, if it ascended into the heart of John (if in any way it can be said), it ascended into his heart in so far as he was not man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means “was not man”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In so far as he had begun to be an angel.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For all saints are angels, since they are messengers of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore to carnal and natural men, who are not able to perceive the things that are of God, what says the apostle?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For whereas ye say, I am of Paul, I of Apollos, are ye not men?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What did he wish to make them whom he upbraided because they were men?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do you wish to know what he wished to make them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear in the Psalms: “I have said, ye are gods; and all of you are children of the Most High.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To this, then, God calls us, that we be not men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But then will it be for the better that we be not men, if first we recognize the fact that we are men, that is, to the end that we may rise to that height from humility; lest, when we think that we are something when we are nothing, we not only do not receive what we are not, but even lose what we are.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, brethren, of these mountains was John also, who said, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This mountain had received peace; he was contemplating the divinity of the Word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of what sort was this mountain?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He had risen above all peaks of the earth, he had risen above all plains of the sky, he had risen above all heights of the stars, he had risen above all choirs and legions of the angels.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For unless he rose above all those things which were created, he would not arrive at Him by whom all things were made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You cannot imagine what he rose above, unless you see at what he arrived.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou inquire concerning heaven and earth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They were made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou inquire concerning the things that are in heaven and earth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Surely much more were they made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou inquire concerning spiritual beings, concerning angels, archangels, thrones, dominions, powers, principalities?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These also were made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when the Psalm enumerated all these things, it finished thus: “He spoke, and they were made; He commanded, and they were created.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If “He spoke and they were made,” it was by the Word that they were made; but if it was by the Word they were made, the heart of John could not reach to that which he says, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God,” unless he had risen above all things that were made by the Word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What a mountain this!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How high among those mountains that received peace for the people of God, that the hills might receive righteousness!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consider, then, brethren, if perchance John is not one of those mountains concerning whom we sang a little while ago, “I have lifted up mine eyes to the mountains, from whence shall come my help.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, my brethren, if you would understand, lift up your eyes to this mountain, that is, raise yourselves up to the evangelist, rise to his meaning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But, because though these mountains receive peace he cannot be in peace who places his hope in man, do not so raise your eyes to the mountain as to think that your hope should be placed in man; and so say, “I have lifted up mine eyes to the mountains, from whence shall come my help,” that you immediately add, “My help is from the Lord, who made heaven and earth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore let us lift our eyes to the mountains, from whence shall come our help; and yet it is not in the mountains themselves that our hope should be placed, for the mountains receive what they may minister to us; therefore, from whence the mountains also receive there should our hope be placed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When we lift our eyes to the Scriptures, since it was through men the Scriptures were ministered, we are lifting our eyes to the mountains, from whence shall come our help; but still, since they were men who wrote the Scriptures, they did not shine of themselves, but “He was the true light, who lighteth every man that cometh into the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A mountain also was that John the Baptist, who said, “I am not the Christ,” lest any one, placing his hope in the mountain, should fall from Him who illuminates the mountain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He also confessed, saying, “Since of His fullness have all we received.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So thou oughtest to say, “I have lifted up mine eyes to the mountains, from whence shall come my help,” so as not to ascribe to the mountains the help that comes to thee; but continue and say, “My help is from the Lord, who made heaven and earth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, brethren, may this be the result of my admonition, that you understand that in raising your hearts to the Scriptures (when the gospel was sounding forth, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God,” and the rest that was read), you were lifting your eyes to the mountains.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For unless the mountains said these things, you would not find out how to think of them at all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore from the mountains came your help, that you even heard of these things; but you cannot yet understand what you have heard.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Call for help from the Lord, who made heaven and earth; for the mountains were enabled only so to speak as not of themselves to illuminate, because they themselves are also illuminated by hearing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thence John, who said these things, received them—he who lay on the Lord’s breast, and from the Lord’s breast drank in what he might give us to drink.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he gave us words to drink.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou oughtest then to receive understanding from the source from which he drank who gave thee to drink; so that thou mayest lift up thine eyes to the mountains from whence shall come thine aid, so that from thence thou mayest receive, as it were, the cup, that is, the word, given thee to drink; and yet, since thy help is from the Lord, who made heaven and earth, thou mayest fill thy breast from the source from which he filled his; whence thou saidst, “My help is from the Lord, who made heaven and earth:” let him, then, fill who can.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Brethren, this is what I have said: Let each one lift up his heart in the manner that seems fitting, and receive what is spoken.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But perhaps you will say that I am more present to you than God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Far be such a thought from you!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is much more present to you; for I appear to your eyes, He presides over your consciences.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give me then your ears, Him your hearts, that you may fill both.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, your eyes, and those your bodily senses, you lift up to us; and yet not to us, for we are not of those mountains, but to the gospel itself, to the evangelist himself: your hearts, however, to the Lord to be filled.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, let each one so lift up as to see what he lifts up, and whither.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What do I mean by saying, “what he lifts up, and whither?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let him see to it what sort of a heart he lifts up, because it is to the Lord he lifts it up, lest, encumbered by a load of fleshly pleasure, it fall ere ever it is raised.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But does each one see that he bears a burden of flesh?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let him strive by continence to purify that which he may lift up to God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “Blessed are the pure in heart, because they shall see God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But let us see what advantage it is that these words have sounded, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We also uttered words when we spoke.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it such a word that was with God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did not those words which we uttered sound and pass away?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did God’s Word, then, sound and come to an end?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If so, how were all things made by it, and without it was nothing made? how is that which it created ruled by it, if it sounded and passed away?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What sort of a word, then, is that which is both uttered and passes not away?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give ear, my beloved, it is a great matter.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By everyday talk, words here become despicable to us, because through their sounding and passing away they are despised, and seem nothing but words.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But there is a word in the man himself which remains within; for the sound proceeds from the mouth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is a word which is spoken in a truly spiritual manner, that which you understand from the sound, not the sound itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Mark, I speak a word when I say “God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How short the word which I have spoken—four letters and two syllables!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is this all that God is, four letters and two syllables?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or is that which is signified as costly as the word is paltry?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What took place in thy heart when thou heardest “God”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What took place in my heart when I said “God”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A certain great and perfect substance was in our thoughts, transcending every changeable creature of flesh or of soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if I say to thee, “Is God changeable or unchangeable?” thou wilt answer immediately, “Far be it from me either to believe or imagine that God is changeable: God is unchangeable.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thy soul, though small, though perhaps still carnal, could not answer me otherwise than that God is unchangeable: but every creature is changeable; how then wert thou able to enter, by a glance of thy spirit, into that which is above the creature, so as confidently to answer me, “God is unchangeable”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, is that in thy heart, when thou thinkest of a certain substance, living, eternal, all-powerful, infinite, everywhere present, everywhere whole, nowhere shut in?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When thou thinkest of these qualities, this is the word concerning God in thy heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But is this that sound which consists of four letters and two syllables?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, whatever things are spoken and pass away are sounds, are letters, are syllables.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His word which sounds passes away; but that which the sound signified, and was in the speaker as he thought of it, and in the hearer as he understood it, that remains while the sounds pass away.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Turn thy attention to that word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou canst have a word in thy heart, as it were a design born in thy mind, so that thy mind brings forth the design; and the design is, so to speak, the offspring of thy mind, the child of thy heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For first thy heart brings forth a design to construct some fabric, to set up something great on the earth; already the design is conceived, and the work is not yet finished: thou seest what thou wilt make; but another does not admire, until thou hast made and constructed the pile, and brought that fabric into shape and to completion; then men regard the admirable fabric, and admire the design of the architect; they are astonished at what they see, and are pleased with what they do not see: who is there who can see a design?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, on account of some great building a human design receives praise, do you wish to see what a design of God is the Lord Jesus Christ, that is, the Word of God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Mark this fabric of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: View what was made by the Word, and then thou wilt understand what is the nature of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Mark these two bodies of the world, the heavens and the earth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who will unfold in words the beauty of the heavens?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who will unfold in words the fruitfulness of the earth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who will worthily extol the changes of the seasons?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who will worthily extol the power of seeds?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You see what things I do not mention, lest in giving a long list I should perhaps tell of less than you can call up to your own minds.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From this fabric, then, judge the nature of the Word by which it was made: and not it alone; for all these things are seen, because they have to do with the bodily sense.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By that Word angels also were made; by that Word archangels were made, powers, thrones, dominions, principalities; by that Word were made all things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence, judge what a Word this is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps some one now answers me, “Who so conceives this Word?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not then imagine, as it were, some paltry thing when thou hearest “the Word,” nor suppose it to be words such as thou hearest them every day—“he spoke such words,” “such words he uttered,” “such words you tell me;” for by constant repetition the term word has become, so to speak, worthless.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when thou hearest, “In the beginning was the Word,” lest thou shouldest imagine something worthless, such as thou hast been accustomed to think of when thou wert wont to listen to human words, hearken to what thou must think of: “The Word was God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now some unbelieving Arian may come forth and say that “the Word of God was made.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How can it be that the Word of God was made, when God by the Word made all things?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the Word of God was itself also made, by what other Word was it made?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if thou sayest that there is a Word of the Word, I say, that by which it was made is itself the only Son of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if thou dost not say there is a Word of the Word, allow that that was not made by which all things were made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For that by which all things were made could not be made by itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Believe the evangelist then.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he might have said, “In the beginning God made the Word:” even as Moses said, “In the beginning God made the heavens and the earth;” and enumerates all things thus: “God said, Let it be made, and it was made.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If “said,” who said?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what was made?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Some creature.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Between the speaking of God and the making of the creature, what was there by which it was made but the Word?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For God said, “Let it be made, and it was made.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This Word is unchangeable; although changeable things are made by it, the Word itself is unchangeable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not then believe that that was made by which were made all things, lest thou be not new-made by the Word, which makes all things new.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For already hast thou been made by the Word, but it behoves thee to be new-made by the Word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, however, thy belief about the Word be wrong, thou wilt not be able to be new-made by the Word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And although creation by the Word has happened to thee, so that thou hast been made by Him, thou art unmade by thyself: if by thyself thou art unmade, let Him who made thee make thee new: if by thyself thou hast been made worse, let Him who created thee re-create thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But how can He re-create thee by the Word, if thou holdest a wrong opinion about the Word?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The evangelist says, “In the beginning was the Word;” and thou sayest, “In the beginning the Word was made.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He says, “All things were made by Him;” and thou sayest that the Word Himself was made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The evangelist might have said, “In the beginning the Word was made:” but what does he say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If He was, He was not made; that all things might be made by it, and without Him nothing be made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, “in the beginning the Word was, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God;” if thou canst not imagine what it is, wait till thou art grown.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That is strong meat: receive thou milk that thou mayest be nourished, and be able to receive strong meat.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give good heed to what follows, brethren, “All things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made,” so as not to imagine that “nothing” is something.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For many, wrongly understanding “without Him was nothing made,” are wont to fancy that “nothing” is something.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Sin, indeed, was not made by Him; and it is plain that sin is nothing, and men become nothing when they sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: An idol also was not made by the Word;—it has indeed a sort of human form, but man himself was made by the Word;—for the form of man in an idol was not made by the Word, and it is written, “We know that an idol is nothing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore these things were not made by the Word; but whatever was made in the natural manner, whatever belongs to the creature, everything that is fixed in the sky, that shines from above, that flies under the heavens, and that moves in universal nature, every creature whatsoever: I will speak more plainly, brethren, that you may understand me; I will say, from an angel even to a worm.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What more excellent than an angel among created things? what lower than a worm?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who made the angel made the worm also; but the angel is fit for heaven, the worm for earth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who created also arranged.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If He had placed the worm in heaven, thou mightest have found fault; if He had willed that angels should spring from decaying flesh, thou mightest have found fault: and yet God almost does this, and He is not to be found fault with.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For all men born of flesh, what are they but worms? and of these worms God makes angels.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if the Lord Himself says, “But I am a worm and no man,” who will hesitate to say what is written also in Job, “How much more is man rottenness, and the son of man a worm?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: First he said, “Man is rottenness;” and afterwards, “The son of man a worm:” because a worm springs from rottenness, therefore “man is rottenness,” and “the son of man a worm.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold what for thy sake He was willing to become, who “in the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God!”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why did He for thy sake become this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That thou mightest suck, who wert not able to chew.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wholly in this sense, then, brethren, understand “All things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For every creature, great and small, was made by Him: by Him were made things above and things beneath; spiritual and corporeal, by Him were they made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For no form, no structure, no agreement of parts, no substance whatever that can have weight, number, measure, exists but by that Word, and by that Creator Word, to whom it is said, “Thou hast ordered all things in measure, and in number, and in weight.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, let no one deceive you, when perchance you suffer annoyance from flies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For some have been mocked by the devil, and taken with flies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As fowlers are accustomed to put flies in their traps to deceive hungry birds, so these have been deceived with flies by the devil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Some one or other was suffering annoyance from flies; a Manichæan found him in his trouble, and when he said that he could not bear flies, and hated them exceedingly, immediately the Manichæan said, “Who made them?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And since he was suffering from annoyance, and hated them, he dared not say, “God made them,” though he was a Catholic.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The other immediately added, “If God did not make them, who made them?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Truly,” replied the Catholic, “I believe the devil made them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the other immediately said, “If the devil made the fly, as I see you allow, because you understand the matter well, who made the bee, which is a little larger than the fly?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Catholic dared not say that God made the bee and not the fly, for the case was much the same.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From the bee he led him to the locust; from the locust to the lizard; from the lizard to the bird; from the bird to the sheep; from the sheep to the cow; from that to the elephant, and at last to man; and persuaded a man that man was not made by God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus the miserable man, being troubled with the flies, became himself a fly, and the property of the devil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In fact, Beelzebub, they say, means “Prince of flies;” and of these it is written, “Dying flies deprive the ointment of its sweetness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then, brethren? why have I said these things?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Shut the ears of your hearts against the wiles of the enemy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Understand that God made all things, and arranged them in their orders.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, then, do we suffer many evils from a creature that God made?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because we have offended God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do angels suffer these things?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps we, too, in that life of theirs, would have no such thing to fear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thy punishment, accuse thy sin, not the Judge.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, on account of our pride, God appointed that tiny and contemptible creature to torment us; so that, since man has become proud and has boasted himself against God, and, though mortal, has oppressed mortals, and, though man, has not acknowledged his fellowman,—since he has lifted himself up, he may be brought low by gnats.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why art thou inflated with human pride?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Some one has censured thee, and thou art swollen with rage.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Drive off the gnats, that thou mayest sleep: understand who thou art.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, that you may know, brethren, it was for the taming of our pride these things were created to be troublesome to us, God could have humbled Pharaoh’s proud people by bears, by lions, by serpents; He sent flies and frogs upon them, that their pride might be subdued by the meanest creatures.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “All things,” then, brethren, “all things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But how were all things made by Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “That, which was made, in Him is life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It can also be read thus: “That, which was made in Him, is life;” and if we so read it, everything is life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what is there that was not made in Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He is the Wisdom of God, and it is said in the Psalm, “In Wisdom hast Thou made all things.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, Christ is the Wisdom of God, and the Psalm says, “In Wisdom hast Thou made all things:” as all things were made by Him, so all things were made in Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, all things were made in Him, dearly beloved brethren, and that, which was made in Him, is life, both the earth is life and wood is life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We do indeed say wood is life, but in the sense of the wood of the cross, whence we have received life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A stone, then, is life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not seemly so to understand the passage, as the same most vile sect of the Manichæans creep stealthily on us again, and say that a stone has life, that a wall has a soul, and a cord has a soul, and wool, and clothing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For so they are accustomed to talk in their raving; and when they have been driven back and refuted, they in some sort bring forward Scripture, saying, “Why is it said, ‘That, which was made in Him, is life’?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if all things were made in Him, all things are life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Be not carried away by them; read thus “That which was made;” here make a short pause, and then go on, “in Him is life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is the meaning of this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The earth was made, but the very earth that was made is not life; but there exists spiritually in the Wisdom itself a certain reason by which the earth was made: this is life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As far as I can, I shall explain my meaning to you, beloved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A carpenter makes a box.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: First he has the box in design; for if he had it not in design, how could he produce it by workmanship?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the box in theory is not the very box as it appears to the eyes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It exists invisibly in design, it will be visible in the work.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, it is made in the work; has it ceased to exist in design?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The one is made in the work, and the other remains which exists in design; for that box may rot, and another be fashioned according to that which exists in design.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give heed, then, to the box as it is in design, and the box as it is in fact.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The actual box is not life, the box in design is life; because the soul of the artificer, where all these things are before they are brought forth, is living.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So, dearly beloved brethren, because the Wisdom of God, by which all things have been made, contains everything according to design before it is made, therefore those things which are made through this design itself are not forthwith life, but whatever has been made is life in Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You see the earth, there is an earth in design; you see the sky, there is a sky in design; you see the sun and the moon, these also exist in design: but externally they are bodies, in design they are life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Understand, if in any way you are able, for a great matter has been spoken.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If I am not great by whom it is spoken, or through whom it is spoken, still it is from a great authority.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For these things are not spoken by me who am small; He is not small to whom I refer in saying these things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let each one take in what he can, and to what extent he can; and he who is not able to take in any of it, let him nourish his heart, that he may become able.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How is he to nourish it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let him nourish it with milk, that he may come to strong meat.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let him not leave Christ born through the flesh till he arrive at Christ born of the Father alone, the God-Word with God, through whom all things were made; for that is life, which in Him is the light of men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this follows: “and the life was the light of men;” and from this very life are men illuminated.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Cattle are not illuminated, because cattle have not rational minds capable of seeing wisdom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But man was made in the image of God, and has a rational mind, by which he can perceive wisdom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That life, then, by which all things were made, is itself the light; yet not the light of every animal, but of men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore a little after he says, “That was the true light, which lighteth every man that cometh into the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By that light John the Baptist was illuminated; by the same light also was John the Evangelist himself illuminated.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was filled with that light who said, “I am not the Christ; but He cometh after me, whose shoe’s latchet I am not worthy to unloose.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By that light he had been illuminated who said, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore that life is the light of men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But perhaps the slow hearts of some of you cannot yet receive that light, because they are burdened by their sins, so that they cannot see.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let them not on that account think that the light is in any way absent, because they are not able to see it; for they themselves are darkness on account of their sins.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And the light shineth in darkness, and the darkness comprehended it not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, brethren, as in the case of a blind man placed in the sun, the sun is present to him, but he is absent from the sun.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So every foolish man, every unjust man, every irreligious man, is blind in heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wisdom is present; but it is present to a blind man, and is absent from his eyes; not because it is absent from him, but because he is absent from it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then is he to do?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let him become pure, that he may be able to see God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just as if a man could not see because his eyes were dirty and sore with dust, rheum, or smoke, the physician would say to him: “Cleanse from your eye whatever bad thing is in it, so that you may be able to see the light of your eyes.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dust, rheum, and smoke are sins and iniquities: remove then all these things, and you will see the wisdom that is present; for God is that wisdom, and it has been said, “Blessed are the pure in heart; for they shall see God.”
